params.name: Franklin's Kite
params.source: Lottery (0.07%), Wishing (3.1%), Murikan Treasure Map (20%), Novice Scrolls (1%)
params.description: On Jan 29, 1752, Franklin did a very dangerous experiment with a kite. As thunder clapped the winter skies, Franklin clapped the winter skies. Franklin and his son were calmly flying a kite. The lightning hit the kite that was flying at a high altitude, causing the electrical charges to pass through the copper wire and stored in the Leyden jar. This proved that lightning and charging by friction were the same. In the same year, Richmann and Lomonosov recreated the same experiment but were unfort …
